Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=櫛稲田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine|qq=}} precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[1142]]), as the {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjusha|qq=}} (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=櫛稲田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of {{ill|Heian Shrine|ja|平安神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Shrine|qq=}}. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}} (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}, with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=櫛稲田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]]

Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]]

It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:聖護院]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

Traffic Shrine: [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:聖護院]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

(eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

(eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[:en:1142|1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[:en:1142|1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyō-ku]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

(eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[:en:1142|1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[:en:1142|1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[:en:1924|1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Dōsojin shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:History of Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Sakyō-ku
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[:en:1142|1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[:en:1142|1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[:en:1924|1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[:en:2005|2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[:en:1142|1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[:en:1142|1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[:en:1924|1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[:en:2005|2005]] ([[Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[Kōji]] 1 ([[:en:1142|1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in the first year of [[Kōji]] ([[:en:1142|1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[:en:1924|1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[:en:2005|2005]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 ([[:en:1142|1142]])
Traffic Shrine: [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = [[May 10]] }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]][[Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]][[Shōgo-in]][[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of [[:en:Yoshida Shrine|Yoshida Shrine]] precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in the first year of [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] ([[:en:1142|1142]]), as the [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]], along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of [[:en:Heian Shrine|Heian Shrine]]. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in [[:en:1924|1924]] ([[Taishō]] 13). In [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[Shōwa]] 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In [[:en:2005|2005]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]

In [[:en:2005|2005]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in [[:en:1758|1758]] ([[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]] * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] }} [[sn:Traffic Shrine]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Liminal deities]]
