[[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.018263111111|N|135.78135888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}}} {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}[[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]{{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}{{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in|1=Shōgo-in}}[[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 ({{ill|1142|en|1142|WD=Q19614|lt=1142|1=1142}})
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]{{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}{{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in|1=Shōgo-in}}[[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|en|Yoshida Shrine|WD=Q713177|lt=Yoshida Shrine|1=Yoshida Shrine}} precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, in the first year of [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] ({{ill|1142|en|1142|WD=Q19614|lt=1142|1=1142}}), as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子|1=Fujiwara no Nariko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5507535}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of {{ill|Heian Shrine|en|Heian Shrine|WD=Q385141|lt=Heian Shrine|1=Heian Shrine}}. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 13). In {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}} in {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758|1=1758}} ({{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17218661}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Liminal deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of {{ill|Heian Shrine|en|Heian Shrine|WD=Q385141|lt=Heian Shrine|1=Heian Shrine}}. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17218661}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Liminal deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.018263111111|N|135.78135888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}}} {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugajinja(Kyoto-shi)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}[[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]{{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}{{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in|1=Shōgo-in}}[[Entomicho]] 1 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 1 | latitude_sec = 6.2 | latNS = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 46 | longitude_sec = 54 | longEW = E | map_type = Japan Kyoto city2 | label_position = left | deity = Suga Shrine: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} | established = Suga Shrine: [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 ({{ill|1142|en|1142|WD=Q19614|lt=1142|1=1142}})
Traffic Shrine: {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 39) | alias = Nishitennōsha | festival = {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} }} '''Suga Shrine・Traffic Shrine''' (須賀神社・交通神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]{{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}{{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in|1=Shōgo-in}}[[Entomicho]]. == History == [[File:吉田神社境内図.png|thumb|Map of {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|en|Yoshida Shrine|WD=Q713177|lt=Yoshida Shrine|1=Yoshida Shrine}} precincts in Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] It was founded in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, in the first year of [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] ({{ill|1142|en|1142|WD=Q19614|lt=1142|1=1142}}), as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} (guardian shrine) of {{ill|Kankikōin|ja|歓喜光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankikōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built by {{ill|Bifukumon-in|ja|美福門院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bifukumon-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原得子|1=Fujiwara no Nariko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5507535}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, along with {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}. The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of {{ill|Heian Shrine|en|Heian Shrine|WD=Q385141|lt=Heian Shrine|1=Heian Shrine}}. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 13). In {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 39), {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, and {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} were separately enshrined, founding Japan's only dedicated '''Traffic Shrine'''. In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 17), the main hall and worship hall were destroyed by fire but swiftly rebuilt. The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. Additionally, a Nishitennōsha invited to the third ward of Fukuoka Castle in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}} in {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758|1=1758}} ({{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 8) was also called "Nishi Tennōsha"Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:須賀神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17218661}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Liminal deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The original location was near Nishitennōzuka, northeast of the Blue Dragon Tower of {{ill|Heian Shrine|en|Heian Shrine|WD=Q385141|lt=Heian Shrine|1=Heian Shrine}}. It was historically known as '''Nishitennōsha''', paired with the {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|lt=Higashitennōsha|lt_ja=東天王社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}}. Later, it was moved to Yoshida-Kaguraoka (currently Yoshida-Kaguraoka-chō, Sakyō-ku, near {{ill|Yamakage Shrine|ja|山蔭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakage Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; see map{{ill|Takao Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿隆男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao Suzuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『吉田探訪誌／平安京を彩る神と仏の里』 (Nakanishiya Shuppan, 2000), p.191), and its current site was established in {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 13). The main shrine now houses Suga Shrine on the left and Traffic Shrine on the right within the same building. It is revered as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} (local birth deity) for the Shōgo-in area and is especially popular for matchmaking, warding off misfortune, and traffic safety. The Setsubun festival attracts numerous visitorsKyoto City information sign. According to the ''Shūi Miyako Meisho Zue'' compiled in the early modern period, the deity worshipped was {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with an annual festival on June 16International Research Center for Japanese Studies, Heian Kyoto Meisho Zue Database. (eds.), ''Chikuzen Machimura Kakigami Chō'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1992), pp.84, 92Hirowatari Masatoshi et al. (eds.), ''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi'' (Bunken Shuppan, 1993), vol. 1, p.200. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Suga Shrine Deities: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} * Traffic Shrine Deities: {{ill|Kunitsunokami|ja|久那斗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsunokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahiko|ja|八衢比古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}}, {{ill|Yachimatahime|ja|八衢比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachimatahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2963653}} == Precincts == * Main Hall (Left: Suga Shrine, Right: Traffic Shrine) * Hakuryū Daimyōjin・{{ill|Inari|ja|稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} Daimyōjin * Shrine Office == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)|version=102053327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q17218661|ja|須賀神社 (京都市)}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Liminal deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shōgoin, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
